gaps.snippet: # [doc = " \\[Declared\\] Read **all** of stdin to end-of-input — **UNBOUNDED, TRUSTED-ONLY**."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This grows the buffer with no cap: a hostile or runaway producer can drive an arbitrarily large"] # [doc = " allocation (memory-exhaustion DoS). Use it **only** where the stdin producer is your own process"] # [doc = " or otherwise under your control. For **untrusted / external-edge** input, use the bounded"] # [doc = " [`read_to_end_capped`] instead (P3 — bounds at the external edge)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns `Err` on any OS read error — never-silent (G2): a partial read that hits an error is"] # [doc = " reported, not truncated-and-returned."] pub fn read_to_end () -> Result < Vec < u8 > , io :: Error > { let mut buf = Vec :: new () ; io :: stdin () . read_to_end (& mut buf) ? ; Ok (buf) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " \\[Declared\\] Read stdin to end-of-input, **bounded by `max` bytes** (P3 — bound at the external"] # [doc = " edge). The safe form for **untrusted** input: a hostile/runaway pipe cannot drive an unbounded"] # [doc = " allocation."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Never-silent (G2): if the input would **exceed** `max` bytes, this returns"] # [doc = " `Err(ReadCappedError::TooLarge { cap: max })` — it never silently truncates the stream. An input"] # [doc = " of **exactly** `max` bytes (or fewer) succeeds and returns those bytes. OS read errors surface as"] # [doc = " `Err(ReadCappedError::Io(_))`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `Declared` — a thin bounded wrapper over `std::io`; no theorem and no measured bound backs the"] # [doc = " underlying stream semantics. The cap itself is exact by construction (the returned buffer length"] # [doc = " is always `<= max`), but the floor's overall guarantee stays `Declared` (VR-5)."] pub fn read_to_end_capped (max : usize) -> Result < Vec < u8 > , ReadCappedError > { read_capped (io :: stdin () . lock () , max) }
gaps.reason: method call `.lock(...)` has no proven-emitted free-fn referent in this file (neither a D4-mangled inherent `Type__lock` nor a bare inherent recorded by this file's left-to-right pass, nor a prim_map / combinator row). Emitting bare `lock(recv, …)` would fabricate an unknown prim and file-poison myc-check (G-β Rank A / G2/VR-5; residual when the method body itself gapped — e.g. M-1037 `.to_vec()` inside `read_to_end` — so the declaration was never registered)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Core bounded read, factored over any [`Read`] so it is unit-testable without real stdin."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Reads up to `max + 1` bytes via [`Read::take`]. Because `.take(max)` would *silently* stop at the"] # [doc = " cap (an invisible truncation — a G2 violation), we instead read one byte past the cap: if the"] # [doc = " result length is `> max`, the input was too large and we return [`ReadCappedError::TooLarge`]"] # [doc = " (explicit, never a truncated buffer). Otherwise the whole input fit within the cap and is"] # [doc = " returned. `max == usize::MAX` is handled without overflow (the `+ 1` saturates)."] fn read_capped < R : Read > (r : R , max : usize) -> Result < Vec < u8 > , ReadCappedError > { let probe = (max as u64) . saturating_add (1) ; let mut buf = Vec :: new () ; r . take (probe) . read_to_end (& mut buf) ? ; if buf . len () > max { return Err (ReadCappedError :: TooLarge { cap : max }) ; } Ok (buf) }
gaps.reason: type parameter `R` carries a bound — type_params/fn generics are bare identifiers only in this grammar fragment

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; # [doc = " The write path returns `Ok` on a normal stdout write and surfaces the byte payload faithfully"] # [doc = " (a smoke test — the OS stream is the ground truth; this only pins that the wrapper does not"] # [doc = " swallow or transform the bytes)."] # [test] fn write_out_and_flush_succeed_on_a_normal_stream () { write_out (b"") . expect ("an empty write succeeds") ; flush_out () . expect ("flush succeeds") ; } # [doc = " Never-silent (G2): `write_all` over a deliberately failing writer surfaces the error rather"] # [doc = " than reporting a phantom success. We exercise the same `Write::write_all` contract `write_out`"] # [doc = " relies on, against a sink that refuses, to pin the never-silent property structurally."] # [test] fn a_failing_write_is_an_explicit_error_not_a_phantom_success () { struct Refuse ; impl Write for Refuse { fn write (& mut self , _ : & [u8]) -> io :: Result < usize > { Err (io :: Error :: other ("refused")) } fn flush (& mut self) -> io :: Result < () > { Ok (()) } } let err = Refuse . write_all (b"x") . expect_err ("a refusing writer must surface an error") ; assert_eq ! (err . kind () , io :: ErrorKind :: Other) ; } # [doc = " P3 / under-cap: an input shorter than the cap is returned in full."] # [test] fn capped_read_under_cap_returns_all_bytes () { let input : & [u8] = b"hello" ; let out = read_capped (input , 64) . expect ("an under-cap read must succeed") ; assert_eq ! (out , b"hello" , "the full payload must be returned unchanged") ; } # [doc = " Boundary: an input of *exactly* the cap length succeeds (the cap is inclusive — `len <= max`)."] # [test] fn capped_read_at_exactly_cap_returns_all_bytes () { let input : & [u8] = b"abcd" ; let out = read_capped (input , 4) . expect ("an at-cap read must succeed") ; assert_eq ! (out , b"abcd") ; } # [doc = " Never-silent (G2): an input that EXCEEDS the cap is an explicit `TooLarge` error — never a"] # [doc = " silently-truncated buffer. This is the DoS-bound test (P3): we must refuse, not truncate."] # [test] fn capped_read_over_cap_is_explicit_error_not_silent_truncation () { let input : & [u8] = b"abcdef" ; match read_capped (input , 4) { Err (ReadCappedError :: TooLarge { cap }) => { assert_eq ! (cap , 4 , "the error must cite the cap") } other => panic ! ("over-cap input must be TooLarge, never a truncated Ok: {other:?}") , } } # [doc = " Edge: a zero cap rejects any non-empty input (one byte already exceeds the cap)."] # [test] fn capped_read_zero_cap_rejects_nonempty_input () { let input : & [u8] = b"x" ; assert ! (matches ! (read_capped (input , 0) , Err (ReadCappedError :: TooLarge { cap : 0 })) , "a 1-byte input must exceed a zero cap") ; } # [doc = " Edge: a zero cap accepts empty input (nothing to read — the bound holds vacuously)."] # [test] fn capped_read_zero_cap_accepts_empty_input () { let input : & [u8] = b"" ; let out = read_capped (input , 0) . expect ("empty input fits a zero cap") ; assert ! (out . is_empty ()) ; } # [doc = " The error type names the cap in its `Display` (auditable message; G2 — the refusal is legible)."] # [test] fn too_large_error_display_cites_the_cap () { let e = ReadCappedError :: TooLarge { cap : 128 } ; assert ! (e . to_string () . contains ("128") , "TooLarge Display must cite the cap, got: {e}") ; } # [doc = " Never-silent (G2): an OS read error before the cap surfaces as `Io`, not a partial `Ok`."] # [test] fn capped_read_surfaces_os_error_not_partial_ok () { struct Boom ; impl Read for Boom { fn read (& mut self , _ : & mut [u8]) -> io :: Result < usize > { Err (io :: Error :: other ("boom")) } } match read_capped (Boom , 16) { Err (ReadCappedError :: Io (e)) => assert_eq ! (e . kind () , io :: ErrorKind :: Other) , other => panic ! ("a failing reader must surface Io, not a phantom Ok: {other:?}") , } } }
